gaps: No test covers the specific UI path of navigating Home → [Device Name] → Device Settings → Software to trigger DFU during an active call — existing test ID 65459432 only covers DFU being blocked with a pop-up during an active call but does not specify the exact navigation path or verify the pop-up message content and dismissal behavior.
No test verifies that the 'Update' button on the Software screen is disabled/greyed out (not just blocked by pop-up) when a call is active, which is a different UX pattern.
No test covers the scenario where a call ends while the DFU pop-up is displayed — verifying whether the update can proceed after call ends without re-navigation.
No test covers the scenario where the user dismisses the pop-up and then navigates away and back to the Software screen while still on the call — verifying the pop-up re-appears consistently.

No test covers this behavior on specific device types (e.g., Voyager Free 60+ with multi-component firmware) where the 'Update' button might behave differently for individual component updates vs. 'Update All'.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-CALL-001: Verify DFU 'Update' button is disabled/greyed out on Software screen during an active call
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app on the paired phone.
Tap the [Device Name] (e.g., Voyager Free 60+ UC) on the Home screen to navigate to Device Settings.
Tap 'Device Settings' → 'Software' (or 'Software & Updates').
Verify the 'Update' button (or 'Update All' button) is enabled and tappable (baseline check).
Place an active call from the paired phone (e.g., dial any number or call voicemail).
While the call is active, navigate back to Poly Studio Mobile → Home → [Device Name] → Device Settings → Software.
Observe the state of the 'Update' button (or 'Update All' button) on the Software screen.
generated_testcases.expected: The 'Update' button (or 'Update All' button) on the Software screen is disabled/greyed out and cannot be tapped while a call is active. The button becomes enabled again only after the call ends.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-CALL-002: Verify DFU pop-up appears with correct message when tapping 'Update' during an active call
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app → Home → [Device Name] → Device Settings → Software.
Place an active call from the paired phone.
While the call is active, tap the 'Update' button (or 'Update All' button) on the Software screen.
Observe the pop-up dialog that appears.
Verify the exact text/message content of the pop-up.
Tap 'OK' or 'Dismiss' on the pop-up to close it.
Verify the Software screen is still displayed and the call remains active.
generated_testcases.expected: A pop-up dialog appears with a message indicating that the firmware update cannot proceed while a call is active (e.g., 'Cannot update during a call. Please end your call to start the update.'). The pop-up includes a dismiss/OK button. After dismissing, the Software screen is shown and the call continues uninterrupted.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-CALL-003: Verify DFU can proceed after call ends while pop-up is displayed (no re-navigation needed)
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app → Home → [Device Name] → Device Settings → Software.
Place an active call from the paired phone.
While the call is active, tap the 'Update' button on the Software screen to trigger the DFU-blocked pop-up.
Do NOT dismiss the pop-up. Keep it displayed on screen.
End the active call from the phone (hang up).
Observe the pop-up behavior — does it auto-dismiss, remain, or change?
If the pop-up remains, tap 'OK'/'Dismiss' to close it.
Tap the 'Update' button again on the Software screen.
Observe whether the firmware update begins.
generated_testcases.expected: After the call ends, the pop-up may auto-dismiss or remain. In either case, tapping 'Update' again on the Software screen should now initiate the firmware update process (download/install begins) without needing to re-navigate. The update should proceed normally.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-CALL-004: Verify DFU pop-up re-appears consistently after dismiss and re-navigation while still on call
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app → Home → [Device Name] → Device Settings → Software.
Place an active call from the paired phone.
While the call is active, tap the 'Update' button on the Software screen.
Verify the DFU-blocked pop-up appears.
Dismiss the pop-up by tapping 'OK' or the close button.
Navigate away from the Software screen (e.g., tap 'Audio Settings' or tap the back arrow to go to Device Settings).
Navigate back to the Software screen: Device Settings → Software.
While still on the active call, tap the 'Update' button again.
Observe whether the pop-up appears again.
Repeat the dismiss-and-re-navigate cycle 3 times.
generated_testcases.expected: The DFU-blocked pop-up appears consistently every time the 'Update' button is tapped while a call is active, regardless of how many times the user dismisses it and re-navigates to the Software screen. The pop-up behavior is deterministic and repeatable.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-CALL-005: Verify DFU blocked for individual component updates on multi-component firmware device (e.g., Voyager Free 60+) during active call
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app → Home → tap 'Voyager Free 60+ UC' → Device Settings → Software.
Verify the Software screen shows multiple firmware components (e.g., 'Earbuds Firmware', 'Charge Case Firmware', 'Touchscreen Firmware') with individual 'Update' buttons and an 'Update All' button.
Place an active call from the paired phone.
While the call is active, tap the individual 'Update' button for the first component (e.g., 'Earbuds Firmware').
Observe the result.
Dismiss any pop-up if shown.
Tap the individual 'Update' button for the second component (e.g., 'Charge Case Firmware').
Observe the result.
Dismiss any pop-up if shown.
Tap the 'Update All' button.
Observe the result.
generated_testcases.expected: All individual component 'Update' buttons AND the 'Update All' button are either disabled/greyed out OR show the DFU-blocked pop-up when tapped during an active call. No component update can be initiated while the call is active. The behavior is consistent across all update entry points.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-CALL-006: Verify DFU blocked pop-up appears when call is received (incoming) while on Software screen
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app → Home → [Device Name] → Device Settings → Software.
Ensure no call is active. Verify the 'Update' button is enabled.
Have a second phone call the paired phone (incoming call).
While the phone is ringing (incoming call), tap the 'Update' button on the Software screen.
Observe the result.
Answer the incoming call.
While the call is now active, tap the 'Update' button again.
Observe the result.
generated_testcases.expected: During the incoming call ringing state, tapping 'Update' may either show the pop-up or proceed (depending on whether ringing is treated as 'in use'). After answering the call, tapping 'Update' consistently shows the DFU-blocked pop-up or the button is disabled.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DFU-CALL-007: Verify DFU button state and pop-up behavior during call hold/swap scenarios
steps: Open Poly Studio Mobile app → Home → [Device Name] → Device Settings → Software.
Place Call A from the paired phone.
While Call A is active, verify the 'Update' button is disabled or shows pop-up when tapped.
Place Call A on hold and answer/switched to Call B (call waiting/swap).
While Call B is active (Call A on hold), navigate to Software screen and tap 'Update'.
Observe the result.
End Call B, resume Call A.
While Call A is active again, tap 'Update' and observe the result.
End Call A completely.
Immediately tap 'Update' and verify the firmware update can proceed.
generated_testcases.expected: The DFU update is blocked (button disabled or pop-up shown) during ANY active call state, including when one call is on hold and another is active. The update is only allowed after ALL calls have ended.
